titleStmt.title: Letter from Hanoria Shanahan to the Lord Lieutenant, Lord Wimborne, 8 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Hanoria Shanahan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
A handwritten petition by Hanoria Shanahan to carry on her business as publican and grocer in a building close to her premises in Portmagee, Co. Kerry which had been destroyed by fire in November 1915.
Lord Wimborne (1873 –1939) was Lord Lieutenant, a largely ceremonial role, from 1915 until 1918.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers 1916, NAI-CSO-RP-1916-5623-646

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Petition To His Excellency
Lord Wimbourne
Lord Lieutenant of Ireland I pray your Excellency to give me permission to carry on my
business as publican and grocer in a small house not twenty
yards from the destroyed premises. I pray your excellency to consider my case as most charitable,
and enable me to support my young family of seven children
(the eldest of which is not 13 years), until I am enabled
to build my house. I pray your excellency to consider my request so
deserving, that the charitable public are providing me
the means of sustenance and the restoration of my little
tradefor the sake of the helpless family. I pray your Excellency not to refuse but to grant — the
petition of a heart broken widow, and may the Almighty
and just lord grant you all the requests you ask of Him
in these troublesome times is my humble prayer. Hanoria Shanahan Portmagee Calterry 8.1.16
